name: AMP Futures
description_short: CFTC-regulated futures broker with ultra-low commissions, $100 minimum deposit, and support for 60+ trading platforms including NinjaTrader, TradingView, and Sierra Chart.
description_long:
AMP Futures (AMP Global Clearing LLC) is a Chicago-based, CFTC-registered Futures Commission Merchant (FCM) and NFA member founded in 2004. Specializing exclusively in futures trading, AMP provides access to global electronic futures markets across CME, CBOT, NYMEX, COMEX, ICE, Eurex, and 45+ exchanges worldwide. The firm serves retail and professional traders from its LaSalle Street headquarters, with AMP Global (Europe) extending services to non-US clients.

AMP's defining advantage is its platform neutrality — with support for over 60 third-party trading platforms, it offers more platform choices than any other futures broker. Traders can connect NinjaTrader, TradingView, Sierra Chart, MetaTrader 5, MultiCharts, Quantower, CQG Desktop, and dozens of others directly to AMP's clearing services. This flexibility allows traders to use their preferred tools without switching brokers or compromising on features.

Commissions are among the industry's lowest. Micro E-mini S&P 500 (MES) contracts trade for approximately $0.44 per contract, and day trading margins start at just $40 for MES and $400 for the full-size ES contract. The minimum account deposit is only $100, making AMP accessible to traders at any account size. High-volume traders can opt for the Unlimited Annual Plan at $1,000/year for commission-free trading. AMP also offers a price-match guarantee on any written commission quote from a competing broker.

AMP is purpose-built for futures traders and does not offer equities, spot forex, or spot crypto under its US entity. There is no proprietary mobile app — mobile trading is handled through partner platforms like MetaTrader 5 and TradingView Mobile. The lean organizational structure keeps overhead costs low, which benefits traders through pricing, though support response times can be inconsistent. Free demo accounts are available across all supported platforms.

tiers.name: Demo Account
features: Simulated access to all futures markets

tiers.name: Live Account
features: $40 day trading margin per MES contract

pros: Supports 60+ trading platforms — more than any other futures broker
Ultra-low commissions starting at ~$0.44/contract for MES futures
Industry-low $100 minimum deposit with no monthly account fees
Exceptionally low day trading margins ($40 MES, $400 ES)
Commission price-match guarantee against any competing broker
cons: No proprietary trading platform or branded mobile app
US entity restricted to futures only — no stocks, spot forex, or spot crypto
$30 wire withdrawal fee and $25 per liquidation occurrence
Small team (11–50 employees) can lead to slower customer support
best_for: micro futures and small account traders
alt_meta_title: Best AMP Futures Alternatives 2026: Lower Fees Exist
